imposition_statement: Agon is not adapted to the current system; the current system is audited for Agon survival.

survival_axes.question: Does the surface help future conflict become reviewable state change rather than chat theater?
survival_axes.owner_guard: Do not let protocol law leak into owner repositories that only provide inputs, proof, memory, routing, or runtime support.

survival_axes.question: Does the surface preserve source-owned meaning instead of letting the center or a derived layer absorb it?
survival_axes.owner_guard: Owner repositories keep their object-class truth; the center names boundaries and constitutional law only.

survival_axes.question: Can the future arena distinguish agonic actors from civil/service assistants before protocol sessions open?
survival_axes.owner_guard: Actor form belongs in aoa-agents; arena law belongs in Agents-of-Abyss.

survival_axes.question: Can claims be inspected through docs, schemas, generated capsules, receipts, validators, or owner references?
Does the surface keep scars, verdicts, and derived summaries in their owning layers instead of fusing them into one authority?
survival_axes.owner_guard: Memo recalls; evals prove; stats derive; none reign alone.

survival_axes.question: Does the surface prevent service assistants from becoming hidden contestants by gradual drift?
survival_axes.owner_guard: Civil/service actor governance must require explicit owner-reviewed recharter before any arena contestant role.

survival_axes.question: Does the surface prevent direct arena-to-Tree canonization?
survival_axes.owner_guard: Arena outcomes may become candidates only through reviewable memory, proof, derived substrate, and ToS-owned judgment.

survival_axes.question: Does the surface avoid claiming live services before protocol and runtime owners have landed contracts?
survival_axes.owner_guard: Doctrine is not runtime; runtime belongs to abyss-stack when the protocol is ready.

owner_handoffs.concern: Agonic actor form and civil/service assistant split
owner_handoffs.boundary: Own role-facing actor form, kind split, office posture, refusal posture, and eligibility; do not own arena protocol, scars, verdicts, runtime packets, or ToS promotion.
owner_handoffs.next_wave: Wave I and Wave II rechartering

owner_handoffs.boundary: Own reusable practice patterns only; do not define lawful arena sessions.
owner_handoffs.next_wave: Wave III language of lawful moves input

owner_handoffs.boundary: Own execution workflows only; admissibility and protocol law remain center-owned.
owner_handoffs.next_wave: Wave III language of lawful moves input

owner_handoffs.boundary: Own thin gates and next-hop hints; do not judge truth or own session lifecycle.
owner_handoffs.next_wave: After actor formation and protocol kernel

owner_handoffs.concern: Trial rituals and early mechanical campaigns
owner_handoffs.boundary: Own recurring scenario choreography; do not become arena sovereignty.
owner_handoffs.next_wave: After protocol kernel and lawful moves

owner_handoffs.boundary: Own proof and bounded verdict surfaces; do not become readiness government.

owner_handoffs.concern: Scars, delta history, and retention recall
owner_handoffs.boundary: Own memory truth; do not certify present health or proof.
owner_handoffs.next_wave: After center protocol kernel and eval verdict surface

owner_handoffs.boundary: Own derived observability; do not become score authority or judge.
owner_handoffs.next_wave: After proof and memory receipts exist

owner_handoffs.boundary: Own derived lifts only; do not replace source meaning or ToS canon.
owner_handoffs.next_wave: After retention and proof-backed survivor candidates exist

owner_handoffs.concern: Typed helper seams and control-plane support
owner_handoffs.boundary: Support protocol consumption only after protocol surfaces exist; do not hide policy in helpers.
Run services only after law, proof, memory, routing, and persistence contracts exist.
Own source-first canonization; no direct arena write path.
owner_handoffs.next_wave: Only after memo, eval, KAG, and ToS-owned review

stop_lines: Do not claim a live Agon runtime in Wave 0.
Do not create a new Agon-of-Abyss sibling repository.
Do not add arena session, lawful move, contradiction ledger, verdict, scar, or retention schemas in Wave 0.
Do not treat release-clean as Agon-ready.
Do not let assistants become hidden contestants.
Do not let stats, evals, memo, routing, SDK, runtime, or KAG become sovereign over Agon.
Do not write directly from arena outcomes into Tree-of-Sophia canon.
next_allowed_waves: Wave I: Agonic Actor Rechartering in aoa-agents
Wave II: Assistant Civil Rechartering in aoa-agents
Wave II.5: Formation Trial before lawful move language
